commit.message:
refactor: split admin list pages into server/client components

All 5 admin list pages crashed because they passed a render prop
function to a client component from a server component (React 19
boundary violation).

Fix: each page now has a thin server page.tsx (data fetch) and a
client.tsx (search + table rendering). AdminSearch refactored from
render prop pattern to useSearch hook + AdminSearchInput component.

commit.message: refactor: admin 100% shadcn/ui — zero custom UI Purged ALL inline styles, custom HTML elements, and manual Tailwind color classes from the entire admin panel. Login: rewritten with shadcn Card, Input, Label, Button, Alert. Sidebar: removed inline style, uses bg-card from CSS variables. Layout: removed inline backgroundColor/fontFamily. Tables: raw replaced with shadcn Avatar + AvatarFallback. Forms: raw replaced with shadcn Checkbox + Label. Delete: variant="destructive" instead of ghost+custom color. FormSelect: raw

fix: simplify 1-on-1 thread detection — prompt-only approach

Instead of detecting 1-on-1 threads at the handler level, rewrite the
mention signal to tell the LLM to check conversation history itself.
If it sees a back-and-forth with one person, respond naturally.

commit.message:
docs: document nap quality adjustment rejection with data

Tested across 6 babies, 2448 nap-to-nap gaps: adjusting wake windows
after short/long naps makes predictions worse. No adjustment wins on
5/6 babies and every duration bucket. "Stick to the plan" is correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Tally name extraction with heuristic fallback + debug endpoint

- Add /api/sgc/ops/debug/tally endpoint (stores last raw Tally payload in memory)
- Try payload.data.fields OR .responses OR .fields (handle Tally structure variants)
- Include f.key in label matching (Tally uses key when label is absent)
- Add nameByHeuristic(): scans all field values for one that looks like a human name
  (letters/spaces only, not a UUID, 2-60 chars) — catches when label+position both fail
- Log full structured fields JSON for Railway diagnosis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add intra-day backtest harness, reject nap quality WW adjustment

New intraday-backtest replays each nap sequentially and predicts
the next gap — the right harness for testing nap-quality hypotheses.

Result: after short naps (≤30m), actual gap averages 183 min — nearly
identical to normal naps (192 min). Adjusting WW after short naps
makes predictions worse (MAE 30.8 vs 30.4). Removed the adjustment.

"Stick to the plan" wins over "react to the short nap." Matches parent
experience and common sleep consultant advice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Swap editor sidebar order: Detalhes da Obra now above Resumo Financeiro

commit.message:
test(samples): add APL test suite with GNU APL and COR24 runners

Add samples/ directory with 9 test suites covering all implemented
features: scalar arithmetic, variables, vectors, iota, rho, reduce,
take/drop, rev/cat, and matrix operations. Each test has three files:
- .apl (GNU APL Unicode syntax with ⎕IO←0)
- .cor24 (COR24 ASCII keyword syntax)
- .expected (expected COR24 output)

Two test runner scripts:
- test-gnu-apl.sh: validates .apl files against local GNU APL
- test-cor24.sh: runs .cor24 files on emulator, diffs with .expected

All 9 COR24 tests pass. GNU APL outputs match semantically.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rebalance score weights so max=100 without artificial cap

Old system: base=50, max positives=75 → raw score could reach 125,
clamped to 100, making penalties invisible when score was very high.

New system: base=10, max positives=90 → theoretical max exactly 100.
Every penalty is always visible in the final score.

Weights:
- RSI:        +18 (was +15)
- EMA trend:  +18 (was +15)
- SMA200:     +12 (was +10)
- Mansfield:  +14 (was +12)
- Volume:     +10 (was +8)
- R:B:        +12 (was +10)
- Analysts:   +6  (was +5)
- Base:        10 (was 50)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add sleep onset latency guidance

Assesses fall-asleep time: <5 min = possibly overtired, 5-20 min = good
timing (Galland 2012 mean: 19 min), >20 min = possibly undertired.
Includes trend summarizer for recent sleeps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add nap quality wake window adjustment

Short nap (≤30 min, 1 sleep cycle) → 15% shorter next wake window.
Long nap (≥90 min, multi-cycle) → 10% longer next wake window.
Exposed as adjustWakeWindowForNapQuality() + optional param on predictNextNap().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add target bedtime backward planning

New planBackwardFromBedtime() works backward from a desired bedtime to
space naps optimally. DB schema adds target_bedtime column to baby table.
Event schema accepts targetBedtime in baby.updated events.

Both forward (follow-the-baby) and backward (target bedtime) modes available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge: integrate BabyContext refactor with prediction improvements

Merges worktree-refactor-baby-context (BabyContext threading through engine)
with the prediction roadmap work (baselines, confidence, calibration, weighted,
transition logic). Adapts all baseline/weighted predictors to BabyContext API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stop demo on user data drop and guard Act 5 particle clear

When a user drops a CSV/GeoJSON file, the demo orchestrator is now stopped
immediately so no subsequent act can overwrite user particles. Act 5's setup()
also checks whether user data exists before clearing the particle buffer,
handling the edge case where data was dropped during an earlier act.

Adds a console.warn when ParticleLayer is null during render to aid debugging
silent failures from map load race conditions.

commit.message:
fix: defer cache loading to prevent MCP client connection timeouts

The server was loading 138MB of persistent cache (7,534 files) from disk
BEFORE setting up the STDIO transport. This blocked the MCP client from
connecting for many seconds, causing repeated "Failed to reconnect"
errors in Claude Code.

Now the STDIO transport is established first (~1 second), and the cache
loads in the background. Tools get cache misses until it's warm, which
is seamless since they fall through to live API calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add calibration tracking for prediction trust signals

Reports which prediction components are learned vs age-default,
data quality (days/naps in lookback), and actionable warnings
(sparse data, transition zones, inconsistent patterns).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add weighted recency predictor (backtest-only comparison)

Exponential decay (0.85x/day) for wake window and nap duration learning.
Result: slightly worse than flat average (60.4 vs 58.3 nap MAE) — confirms
the doc's assessment that weighted recency has low reward with current data.
Kept as a backtest comparison tool for re-evaluation with more datasets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace hard nap count mode switch with recency-weighted scoring

During nap transitions (e.g. 2→1), keeps both hypotheses alive and
uses exponential recency weighting (0.8x decay) to pick the best one.
Adapts faster than the old >60% dominance threshold.

Results on Halldis: 78% → 80% nap count accuracy (6mo: 20→60%, 9mo: 72→83%).
Engine now beats all trivial baselines on nap count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: psychology-grounded engagement engine (OCEAN, Reactance, ELM, Cialdini)

Rewrite should_engage() with research-backed behavioral modifiers:

- Big Five / OCEAN (Correa 2010): extraversion boosts engagement volume,
  agreeableness amplifies herd behavior susceptibility, neuroticism
  amplifies negative engagement, openness boosts novelty-seeking
- Reactance Theory (Brehm 1966): detect hard-sell pressure in content
  (scarcity, urgency, dogmatism signals), trigger hostile pushback in
  reactance-prone personas. Reactance = MORE engagement, but negative.
- ELM Processing (Petty & Cacioppo 1986): central processors engage less
  often but more meaningfully, peripheral processors react to surface cues
- Cialdini persuasion pressure scoring: count hard-sell signals in content
  text to estimate how aggressively persuasive the material is

Also enriched LLM context:
- System prompt now instructs LLM on how to use each persona's Big Five
  traits, processing style, and reactance state when generating reactions
- Reactance flag passed to LLM when a persona's BS detector fires
- Persona batch includes psychology profile for LLM reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve login shell PATH for server subprocess on macOS GUI apps

The #1 remaining blocker: Electron GUI apps on macOS have a minimal
PATH that doesn't include nvm/fnm/homebrew paths. The server subprocess
inherits this limited PATH, so verifyClaudeCode() can't find 'claude'.

Fix: before spawning the server, resolve the full login shell PATH via
$SHELL -lc 'echo $PATH' and pass it to the subprocess env. This means
the server sees the same PATH as the user's terminal.

Also added verbose logging to verifyClaudeCode() login shell fallback
for debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add confidence intervals for sleep predictions

Computes ±1 SD ranges from lookback wake window and nap duration variance.
Uncertainty compounds for later naps. Falls back to age-bracket ranges
when data is sparse. Levels: high (<20 min SD), medium (<40), low (≥40 or <3 days data).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: unmarshal data-wrapped responses before trying direct unmarshal

When T is a slice type, unmarshaling {"data":[...]} directly into []T
fails because the JSON is an object not an array. Try the data wrapper
first, then fall back to direct unmarshal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix docker permission denied in incus containers

incus.shell: pass supplementary groups (e.g. docker) via --group flags
by querying `id -G` inside the container before exec. The previous
--user/--group change only set the primary GID, dropping group
memberships needed for docker socket access.

incus.init: replace `sh -c 'bash -s'` with `sh -s` in install_docker
and install_chromium so heredoc blocks actually execute on Alpine,
which doesn't have bash by default.

commit.message:
fix: TZ-correct day grouping in learning, skip bedtime on no-nap days

Two codex findings:

1. getLearnedNapCount and getPositionalWakeWindows grouped sleeps by
   UTC date (start_time.slice(0,10)) while the app uses the baby's
   timezone for day boundaries. Now uses isoToDateInTz() throughout.
   TZ parameter threaded through getWakeWindow, predictDayNaps, and
   all internal learning functions.

2. Backtest scored bedtime on no-nap days where the predictor falls
   back to "19:00 today" using new Date() (wrong date for historical
   data). Now skips bedtime scoring when no naps exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: suppress literal "No response." text when LLM skips noResponse tool

When allowNoResponse is true and the LLM outputs "No response." as
literal text instead of calling the noResponse tool, the bot was posting
that text to the thread. Now we detect this pattern and suppress it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 3 failing ArcGIS county endpoints: Cuyahoga, Kent, Summit

Browse each county's services directory and discovered the correct service paths:

- Cuyahoga: Parcels/MapServer → CCGIS/Parcels_CAMA_Real_Property/MapServer/3
  Fields: parcelpin, par_addr_all, par_city, par_zip, certified_tax_total,
  total_res_liv_area, parcel_owner

- Kent: Parcels/MapServer → ParcelsWithCondos/MapServer/0
  Fields: PNUM, PROPERTYADDRESS, PROPADDRESSCITY, PROPADDRESSSTATE_ZIPCODE,
  SEVTRIBUNAL1 (×2 for market value), OWNERNAME1

- Summit: Parcels/MapServer → Maps/ParcelQuery/MapServer/0
  Fields: OwnershipTable_SERIAL, OwnershipTable_SITUS, OwnershipTable_TAXVAL,
  OwnershipTable_OWNER

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(auth): use VERCEL_URL for dynamic base URL

Fix CORS issue by using Vercel's VERCEL_URL environment variable
which is automatically set to the current deployment's domain.
This ensures preview deployments use their own domain instead of
the production domain for auth requests.

- Development: localhost:3000
- Vercel (any environment): uses VERCEL_URL automatically
- Fallback: BETTER_AUTH_URL if set

This fixes auth requests failing with CORS on preview deployments.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)
